_description: Default arm set for AuroraBanditAgent. Mirrors the hardcoded grid in ActionGrid::load_default(). Each arm = a partial setting of GEOPM controls; unspecified knobs are left at the previous tick's value. Originally derived from Phase 0 winners per workload class (see analysis/agent_suggestions.md §3); revised after Phase 1.5 Block A validation (see analysis/phase1_5/results.md).
_phase1_5_changes: A2 gpu_compute_max: CPU/UNCORE raised to MAX (was 2.0/1.6 GHz). Reason: GPU-active benches still need CPU at MAX for kernel dispatch — the old A2 added +27% runtime on dgemm-gpu. A6 bursty_gpu_idle: marked _inactive (dominated by A4 on every safe-class bench in Phase 1.5 data; reactivate after Phase 1.5b runs Block C/D/E).
_phase1_5b_findings: Block B (CPU × GPU 3×3) confirms ADDITIVITY on 5/7 benches; small synergy on osu, small antagonism on stream. Agent grid is validated as-is — no new arms needed. Block C partial: cpu-dgemm has a compound bottleneck on (CPU=1.0 + UNCORE=0.8) — same pattern as A4/A7. The agent's runtime guard now penalizes BOTH A4 and A7 when cpu_power_frac > 0.5.
_phase1_5c_findings: Block C COMPLETE (5/7 benches ADDITIVE on CPU×UNCORE). Block D nearly complete (PERF_FACTOR NOT redundant — PF=1 saves 6-22% more energy on most benches at GPU=0.4 GHz, BUT PF=1 HURTS dgemm-gpu at GPU=MAX with +14% energy and +21% runtime). PF settings flipped: A2 gpu_compute_max changed from PF=1.0 to PF=0.0 (memory-bias feeds compute on GPU-active workloads); A4/A5/A7 changed from PF=0.0 to PF=1.0 (compute-bias is better when GPU is capped low because it minimizes idle-clock dither). A1 memory_bound_save kept at PF=0.0 (Block D shows marginal effect on stream/babelstream).
_phase1_5d_findings: Block E (CPU×CPU_POWER_LIMIT) shows CPU_PL IS REDUNDANT with CPU_FREQ_MAX on 5/6 benches when CPU is already capped at 1.0 GHz (|diff| ≤ 1.7%). Sixth bench (cpu-dgemm) sees +338% energy from adding PL=175W on top — A4/A7 weren't safe on cpu-dgemm anyway. CPU_POWER_LIMIT_CONTROL writes DROPPED from A4 (was 175W) and A7 (was 105W). Net effect: simpler arms, same energy savings on target classes, no catastrophic interaction risk.

arms.name: gpu_compute_max
arms._phase1_5_note: CPU/UNCORE were 2.0/1.6 GHz; raised to MAX after Phase 1.5 showed +27% runtime on dgemm-gpu. GPU benches still need CPU at MAX for kernel dispatch over MPI.
arms._phase1_5c_note: PERF_FACTOR was 1.0; changed to 0.0. Block D showed PF=1 at GPU=MAX HURTS dgemm-gpu by +14% energy and +21% runtime — PF=0 (memory-bias) feeds the compute units instead of starving them. With PF=0 + everything at MAX, A2 is now barely distinct from A0 (only the explicit PF setting differs) — keep for traceability and as a hedge.

arms.name: comm_wait_save
arms._phase1_5c_note: PERF_FACTOR was 0.0; changed to 1.0. Block D showed PF=1 at GPU=0.4 GHz saves 19% MORE energy on mpi-idle-wait (the bench this arm targets). Mechanism: PF=1 keeps the small amount of GPU work at high clock, finishes faster, more time in deep idle.
arms._phase1_5d_note: CPU_POWER_LIMIT_CONTROL=175W was dropped. Block E showed PL is redundant with CPU_FREQ=1.0 GHz on 5/6 benches (≤1.7% diff) and CATASTROPHIC on cpu-dgemm (+338% energy from PL alone). Removing the write makes the arm simpler and removes the cpu-dgemm risk.

arms.name: comm_collective_safe
arms._phase1_5_note: UNIVERSAL SAFE ARM — Phase 1.5 showed −13 to −28% energy on 5 of 7 benches at <5% runtime cost (only fails on babelstream/dgemm-gpu where all arms fail). Use as the cold-start default in always-on mode; demote A0 (all_max) to safety-fallback only.
arms._phase1_5c_note: PERF_FACTOR was 0.0; changed to 1.0. Block D shows PF=1 at GPU=0.4 saves 9% MORE on osu and 16% MORE on stream — both targets of this arm.

arms.name: bursty_gpu_idle
arms._phase1_5_note: Dominated by A4 (comm_wait_save) on every safe-class bench in Phase 1.5: gpu-bursty −43% vs −53%, mpi-idle −45% vs −70%. Kept in the JSON for Phase 1.5b reactivation when Block C/D/E data lands.

arms.name: aggressive_save
arms._phase1_5b_note: Block C revealed compound bottleneck: (CPU=1.0 + UNCORE=0.8) gives +76% runtime on cpu-dgemm vs +38% predicted. Same family as A4. The agent's runtime guard penalizes A7 when cpu_power_frac > 0.5; explicitly avoid this arm on CPU-bound classes.
arms._phase1_5c_note: PERF_FACTOR was 0.0; changed to 1.0 (same Block D rationale as A4/A5).
arms._phase1_5d_note: CPU_POWER_LIMIT_CONTROL=105W KEPT. Block E only tested PL=175W (50% TDP) redundancy — it was redundant when CPU=1.0 GHz. PL=105W (30% TDP) is more aggressive and may still bind even at CPU=1.0 GHz, so we keep it pending direct test. This is also what distinguishes A7 from A4 (which has no PL write).
